text_content: As per IEC 62304:2006 Clause 5.1.9 the following controls are in place: 1) the classes, types, categories or lists of items to be controlled: a) The software system is Class C because serious injury is possible should the system  fail to maintain functionality associated with the device’s essential performance. b) Software items within the system have not been de‐rated to B or A, as this is a legacy device with full risk management and V&V coverage. 2) the software configuration management activities and tasks: a) Configuration is managed as per: L9043 Software Configuration Management L6954 Software Versioning 3) the organizations responsible: a) VMS Palo Alto, California, is responsible for all updates to the device software. 4) the relationships with other organizations: a) There are no relationships with other organizations. 5) when items are to be placed under configuration controls

title: 1. Maintenance Release Plan > 1.5 Software Configuration Management
text_content: The Software Configuration Management process in this section will be defined by the Development Operations team (DevOps@varian.com) during Elaboration. Infrastructure, Repositories, and Branching Strategy are defined in the following sections. For HAL 4.0, we have migrated all the console, embedded, NLS, and system builds from legacy XAML builds into TFS Azure Build Pipelines (vNext). For HAL 4.0, we will provide a centralized deployment tool that upgrades all console software from a centralized location. • Formal builds will be available at: \\pa-netapp10\pa_tfsbuild\HAL • Released software will be in Edgecast: https://pse.oscs.varian.com/

title: 1. Introduction
text_content: This document is intended to record the product and process standards that may apply to this device.  The product platform Hal 3.0 supports two product models:  Halcyon and Ethos.  Both models are medical linear accelerators with image guided radiation therapy (IGRT) capabilities. Ethos has an additional capably of adaptive treatment solution. The completed form presents a list of the standards requiring test reports as evidence of compliance unless otherwise indicated. Note:  Additional standards may apply to components used in the medical system or within the medical device. This form is not intended to identify each of these standards.

source: CH2.2 D96379 NMPA Change Description Rev01.docx.pdf
title: 3. Device Changes > 3.1. Hardware changes
text_content: The newly added Halcyon Evolve model does not involve any changes to the hardware. The update consists solely of a configuration adjustment. Specifically, the Halcyon Evolve is equipped only with the standard kV flat panel detector, while the Halcyon model has two kV flat panel detector options, both standard and high-performance, and MV only config. The detailed change in HW configurations can be found in below table. Difference Previous Current The configurations of Halcyon are Hardware classified depending on the kV detector used: Configuration One: MV Imaging and Standard Imaging kV Flat Panel Detector Configuration Two: MV Imaging and High-performance Imaging kV Flat Panel Detector The Configurations of Halcyon and Halcyon Evolve are classified depending on the Imaging options: HalcyonHalcyon Evolve Configuration One: MV Imaging only / Configuration Two: MV Imaging and Standard Imaging kV Flat Panel Detector Configuration Two: MV Imaging and Standard Imaging kV Flat Panel Detector

text_content: Clinical Evaluation Report (EU) HAL 4.0 Revision Doc Number 05 D44094 Varian Confidential CLN0203T01 Rev 01 This document contains confidential, proprietary information of Varian, a Siemens Healthineers Company. It may not be copied or reproduced without prior written permission from Varian. Page 14 of 91 Varian Confidential Product Requirements Addressed by the CER Radiotherapy delivered by linear accelerator is a well-established clinical procedure. HAL was a new model linac in 2017 and it differs from prior models (such as the TrueBeam linac) and from prior Halcyon CERs (e.g., D36038 Rev 3). Listed here and described in more detail in following sections, differences between the device under evaluation, Halcyon 4.0, and the equivalent device, Halcyon 3.1, have been investigated for this clinical evaluation. Product Features and Requirements addressed by this CER include  On-couch Adaptive Treatments  Patient Motion Monitoring  Extended Treatment Field  Extended Imaging 
